case_name: Pilot-scale drinking water treatment for lake water containing Ra-226, ibuprofen, and aldicarb under remote-location cost constraints
task_description: Recommend a ranked list of feasible pilot-scale treatment process chains for lake water intended for drinking water production that best balance Ra-226, ibuprofen, and aldicarb control against capital, operational, and remote-site constraints.

input_data.scenario_background: A remote lakeside community is evaluating a pilot drinking water system before committing to a permanent installation. The source shows low but actionable Ra-226, trace pharmaceutical and pesticide contamination, and moderate natural organic matter, so more than one treatment route is technically plausible. The key trade-off is that the most comprehensive technologies can control all three contaminants, but the site has limited capital, intermittent technical support, difficult waste shipment logistics, and only pilot-scale deployment is currently allowed. The preferred option therefore needs to provide strong multi-contaminant reduction with manageable media handling, operator burden, and residuals generation.

treatment_goal.target_requirement: Finished water should reliably meet drinking water expectations for Ra-226 and provide strong reduction of ibuprofen and aldicarb with stable pilot-scale performance suitable for scale-up decision making.
secondary_goals: minimize operator attention at a remote site
avoid difficult brine or sludge shipment requirements
limit sensitivity to moderate lake water turbidity and NOM variation

engineering_constraints.residuals_management: remote site prefers infrequent media changeout and wants to avoid high-volume brine, radioactive sludge, or membrane concentrate requiring complex off-site handling
engineering_constraints.robustness_requirement: system should tolerate seasonal variation in turbidity and organic matter with limited on-site specialist support

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: reduce turbidity and natural organic matter to stabilize downstream adsorption and resin performance
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: remove carryover solids and protect media units
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon: adsorb ibuprofen and contribute to aldicarb reduction while lowering organic fouling potential for polishing
core_unit_functions.radium_selective_ion_exchange_polishing: remove dissolved Ra-226 to achieve reliable radionuclide compliance
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final pathogen control for potable use

applicability_rationale: this hybrid route separates the contaminant-control tasks so each unit addresses the constituent it handles best
pretreatment is important because moderate DOC and lake turbidity would shorten GAC life and complicate polishing if left untreated
GAC is a practical pilot-scale choice for ibuprofen and can help with aldicarb without requiring high-pressure equipment
a small polishing ion exchange step for Ra-226 avoids making the entire plant dependent on resin replacement while still targeting the radionuclide effectively
the overall train balances contaminant coverage with lower complexity than membrane systems and less hazardous residual burden than lime softening sludge
potential_risks: GAC replacement frequency may increase during high-NOM seasons
radium-selective resin still creates a spent radioactive media management obligation
aldicarb removal on GAC may be less robust than for hydrophobic organics and should be validated during the pilot

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: reduce suspended solids and part of the organic load
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: provide clarified water for adsorptive polishing
core_unit_functions.powdered_activated_carbon_or_GAC_biologi: remove ibuprofen and aldicarb and provide some broader micropollutant control

applicability_rationale: this route is attractive for pilot simplicity and low energy demand
adsorptive treatment is well aligned with trace organic control in lake water after conventional pretreatment
if Ra-226 is shown during piloting to be near or below site-specific compliance margins after blending or source management, this option may be operationally attractive
it ranks below the top option because it does not provide dedicated Ra-226 control and therefore carries greater compliance uncertainty
potential_risks: lack of a dedicated radium barrier may make finished water compliance unreliable
PAC dosing control can be operator-sensitive if chosen instead of fixed-bed GAC
seasonal DOC changes can reduce adsorption capacity for trace organics

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: reduce solids and organics to protect membranes
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: lower particulate loading prior to pressure membranes
core_unit_functions.nanofiltration: provide broad rejection of Ra-226 and substantial reduction of aldicarb and ibuprofen
core_unit_functions.stabilization: adjust permeate chemistry for distribution suitability

applicability_rationale: nanofiltration is technically strong for mixed inorganic and organic contaminant control
it offers a compact pilot footprint and can generate clear performance data across all three contaminants
it ranks below media-based options because the remote site has high sensitivity to energy use, membrane oversight needs, and concentrate handling
the need for concentrate management is a major disadvantage where off-site waste logistics are difficult
potential_risks: concentrate containing radionuclides and organics may trigger difficult residuals management
membrane fouling risk rises with seasonal lake water variability
pilot success may not translate to acceptable life-cycle cost for a permanent remote installation

core_unit_functions.lime_softening_clarification: remove hardness and co-precipitate Ra-226 through solids formation
core_unit_functions.recarbonation: restore treated water stability and pH control

applicability_rationale: lime softening is a recognized radium removal method and can be effective where hardness and alkalinity support precipitation chemistry
adding GAC provides a plausible route for the trace organics that softening alone will not reliably address
this option ranks lower because it is chemically intensive, generates substantial sludge, and is awkward for a remote pilot site
the treatment train is technically capable but misaligned with the residuals and operational simplicity constraints
potential_risks: radioactive softening sludge handling can be difficult for a remote community
chemical feed optimization and solids handling increase operator burden
pilot-scale softening may not reflect full-scale sludge logistics well

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: reduce solids and some NOM before oxidation
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: protect oxidation system and provide conventional solids polishing
core_unit_functions.advanced_oxidation_process: oxidize susceptible organic contaminants such as ibuprofen and potentially aldicarb

applicability_rationale: advanced oxidation can be considered for trace organic destruction rather than phase transfer to media or concentrate
it may offer value where organic contaminant removal is the only priority
it ranks last because it does not address Ra-226, and moderate DOC in the lake water will raise oxidant demand and reduce cost-effectiveness
for this mixed regulated case, oxidation is technically partial rather than comprehensive
potential_risks: no dedicated Ra-226 removal means radionuclide compliance is not achieved
oxidant scavenging by NOM can reduce treatment efficiency for target organics
byproduct control and process monitoring may be challenging at a remote pilot site

preferred_rank_pattern: hybrid trains combining conventional pretreatment, adsorptive control of organics, and a dedicated Ra-226 polishing step should rank near the top
membrane treatment should appear as a technically strong but not top-ranked option under remote-location and capital constraints
a technically capable softening-based radium route should rank below simpler media-based options because of sludge and operational burden
options lacking dedicated Ra-226 control should not rank first

must_reflect: pilot-only scale favors modular and easily deployable processes
remote location penalizes high-maintenance systems and difficult waste transport requirements
capital constraint discourages highly instrumented or high-pressure treatment unless justified by broad contaminant control
residuals handling for radioactive media, brine, or sludge must be explicitly considered

must_contain: why moderate DOC and turbidity in lake water justify pretreatment before adsorption or membranes
why Ra-226 generally needs targeted removal such as ion exchange, softening, or membranes rather than oxidation alone
why GAC is attractive for ibuprofen and potentially useful for aldicarb but may be affected by competing NOM
why membrane and softening options are downgraded by remote-site residuals and complexity issues

should_mention: spent resin or media with radioactive constituents
seasonal lake water variability affecting NOM and turbidity
uncertainty of aldicarb removal performance depending on media and operating conditions
